reasons: Popol and Kupa's value depends on the Popol-Kupa unit relationship, traps, and ongoing kit synergy.
Valentina copying only the ultimate is less threatening than copying a simple high-impact teamfight ultimate.

proof.summary: Valentina wants to copy a high-impact ultimate, while Popol and Kupa's full value depends on Kupa, traps, and kit synergy that is harder to reproduce through only the copied ultimate.
worksBestWhen: Popol uses traps to control Valentina's dash paths.
The team avoids giving Valentina easier high-impact ultimates from other heroes.
failureCases: Valentina copies a stronger ultimate from another enemy.
Popol is caught before trap control matters.
Valentina snowballs through lane pressure instead of copy value.

reasons: Natan's value depends on sustained attack pattern, scaling, and Reverse-Clone synergy.
Valentina's copied ultimate does not automatically give her Natan's full attack-speed DPS identity.

proof.summary: Valentina wants copied ultimates that give immediate fight impact, while Natan's ultimate value is tied to his own basic-attack and clone DPS pattern.
worksBestWhen: Natan keeps spacing and forces Valentina to fight at range.
Natan's team avoids giving Valentina better ultimates to copy.
failureCases: Valentina copies a stronger ultimate from another hero.
Valentina wins early skirmishes before Natan has damage.

reasons: Fanny's real value depends heavily on cable mobility and Prey Mark setup, not only the ultimate button.
Valentina copying Cut Throat alone is less valuable without Fanny's full mobility kit.

proof.summary: Valentina wants easy copied-ultimate value, while Fanny's ultimate depends on setup that Valentina cannot fully reproduce without Steel Cable and Fanny's full kit.
worksBestWhen: Fanny pressures Valentina from unexpected angles.
The draft avoids giving Valentina easier copied ultimates.
failureCases: Fanny misplays energy or cable path.
Valentina copies a stronger ultimate from another hero.
Valentina catches Fanny after she commits.

reasons: Aamon's ultimate is less reliable as a copied tool because its best damage depends on shard setup and target HP condition.
Aamon can pressure Valentina as a burst assassin when she is isolated.

proof.summary: Valentina wants a copied ultimate that works immediately, while Aamon's Endless Shards gains value from shard setup and execution timing.
worksBestWhen: Aamon attacks Valentina after she uses mobility.
The team avoids giving Valentina stronger copy targets.
failureCases: Aamon fails to build execution pressure.

reasons: Paquito's value comes from repeated skill chaining and passive-enhanced combos, not only his ultimate.
This makes copied ultimate value more execution-dependent than simple teamfight ultimates.

proof.summary: Valentina wants simple high-impact ultimates, while Paquito's threat depends on full combo rhythm that copied ultimate alone does not reproduce.
worksBestWhen: Paquito pressures Valentina before she controls the lane.
The team avoids drafting easy copied ultimates beside him.
failureCases: Valentina copies a stronger ultimate from another hero.
Valentina pokes him down before he commits.
